reviews.title: Bad experience with the front desk
reviews.text:
The stay in the hotel was fine. The information from the front desk was terrible. 

They spoke a few times about a storm who was coming, the reason why it was impossible to go on a boat to explore ha long bay. 

When we asked for bus information the couldn’t give us information but sent us by taxi to the bus station. There was exactly the same reaction. Tommorow no busses because of the storm. Back in the hotel we searched on the internet and we find out it wasn’t a storm but a typhoon who was coming, destination Ha Long bay. 

After reading about this on the internet, saw all the warnings from the embassy’s, we decide to book the first flight far away to Ho Chi Min City and skipped our last night in Ha Long Bay.

After all the storm not became to Ha Long Bay but a few 100 kilometers north, but when they are speaking about a storm, a real was, next time warn us! In other situations maybe we got stuck over there..

So as a recap, the hotel itself was fine, the rooms were clean and the beds were fine. The information from the front desk could be much better. We just sleep one night instead of 2.

reviews.title: A real boutique and gem in Halong Bay
reviews.positives:
A rather modern hotel. Around a fifteen minute walk to Vincourt Plaza, which is a shopping mall with a huge supermarket inside. Also around a fifteen minute walk to see the limestone structures. A big shoutout and thank you to Ngoc for her assistance and communication in English and Anne who speaks pretty good Chinese. In addition to that, I wanted to book a trip on the boat to see the limestone structures, Ngoc was REALLY competent and informative on delivering the most effective solution for me, thumbs for that!

The room was roomy, airy, the bed extremely comfy with a good size. The water pressure could be better. All in all a VERY pleasant experience.
reviews.negatives: If I REALLY had to mention, I'd say the hotel is on the side of Halong Bay where the beach isn't really accessible. One has to grab a Grab car to Bai Chay island where most of the action is. The side of the island where the hotel is located doesn't offer much, not that this is a major downside, but if you want to go to the beach and have lots of fun, you need to get a taxi, and unfortunately the bus system in Halong Bay is not really functional, I think.

reviews.title: What you see is what you get.
reviews.text: Outside the main city area in a relatively quiet area. Ha Long walking street nearby but just shops only. Staff are great. Very helpful. Kombo restuarant next door. One major issue for westerners is the breakfast. The ' western' breakfast is just toast, butter, marmalade or jam and fried eggs ( that have a v. strange taste plus the bread is sweet). Coffee, tea, fruit juice, no milk at any of the six days I stayed, even no milk in the room dried or otherwise. . Nothing more. You can have the Vietnamese breakfast but for many westerners it may not suit. This is the hotels only downfall so, if there is a choice, don't book breakfast, find somewhere else, which, in this area, seems pretty difficult but it is Vietnam.  Round bed is uncomfortable tbh..

reviews.title: EVERYTHING'S DECENT BUT THE FOOD
reviews.text: Generally good for the price. Clean, strong wi-fi. DO NOT go for the round bed - it's made of several pieces and exceptionally uncomfortable. Get the 1-piece KING only, do not let them push two twins together. Breakfast was terrible, and the exact same thing every day - noodle soup that smelled like cleaning fluid and overcooked eggs with under-toasted bread. Only thing worthwhile was the coffee - strong and potent. For whatever reasons, this part of the city is dead - hard to get taxis, virtually impossible to find open restaurants. Looked very much like a Chinese "ghost city." After a LOT of looking we found one restaurant we really liked - called Nom 3 Mien. Authentic home cooking. There is a large indoor market within walking distance, and an adjacent open-air food market with tons of fresh fish, veggies, fruits, etc. But no place to cook, so you'll have to settle for the vegetables. Forget about Grab food delivery - it's essentially non-existent. You can order, but all you'll get 20 minute later is "sorry no drivers." The restaurant attached to the hotel is basically fast food - Singapore rice dishes with a couple of good salads. Fine for a day or two, but you get tired of it very fast. It's possible there are high-end restaurants down near the waterfront at night, but we never found any that were still in business or actually open. Don't know whether this is seasonal, Covid related, or just this part of the city.

reviews.title: Una estancia muy cómoda con un standar de limpieza genial
reviews.positives:
Muy nuevo, bien renovado y extremadamente limpio, con diferencia. Un placer haber estado hospedado aquí 
Personal muy asequible con nivel de inglés suficiente para entenderse y hacer una comunicación fluida
La habitación está muy bien equipada y dispone de botellas de agua (pequeñas) y limpieza a diario
La localización está a unos 15 min a pie del mirador de la bahía de Ha Long y del mall principal 
Rodeado de negocios, restaurantes, bancos y en general todo lo que necesitas sin ser “artificial” por lo que a mí me valió la pena disfrutar del hotel y sus alrededores (vecinos de barrio y personas muy reales)
Desayuno incluido y ascensor para moverse por el hotel
Aire acondicionado, ventilador y pequeño frigorífico que hace su función perfectamente
reviews.negatives:
El desayuno se hace “un poco repetitivo” (por decir algo) realmente es una apreciación personal, por lo demás muy bien
El área no es la que se usa para los cruceros y subir (por ejemplo) al teleférico y eso supone desplazarse en Grab o taxi para disfrutar de estas dos posibilidades (entre 6€ y 10€ por viaje dependiendo de la hora)

reviews.title: Good Hotel
reviews.text:
The hotel looks a little nicer in the pictures than in person, but it was still a comfortable stay.   The front desk staff were very friendly and helpful, but there were a couple things I didn't like. 

#1 - Breakfast ends at 9:30 AM, so if you tend to sleep in on vacations you probablly won't be eating the breakfast. 

#2 - The cleaning staff ring your doorbell in the morning at 9:00-9:30 even if you have the "Do not disturb" sign up.   Again, if you tend to sleep in when you are on vacation, this will bother you by waking you up. 

Even with the two things I didn't like, for the price I paid, this was a very good deal.   I would recommend this hotel to people who want a nice vacation while traveling on a budget.

reviews.title: Great hotel for just sleeping, location not ideal
reviews.text:
This is a good hotel, it was clean, the staff were friendly,  and it was located next to a convenience store and not far from the wheel, and some cute cafes overlooking the bay - only a few minutes via a scooter. 

It wasn't a perfect location for me since the nearby tourist attraction is officially closed off for group (where tourists would climb up to this mountain to get some good views), and we weren't that interested in the big wheel, since many cities have one, and many others offer better views, etc. 

In the end, the best location would be next to the harbor for public ferry, and for private tours. 

Facilities were also lacking, but that's only because we were looking for a gym or a rooftop bar that would be busier. Because it was cold, and there weren't that many guests, that was closed so there goes that star, and there are no extras, such as a gym or a pool, so there's the other star for facilities.

reviews.title: A Great Budget Accommodation
reviews.text: We booked 2 nights at the hotel in Hà Long. Were out most of the time and spent 1 full day at the Bay. The hotel offers a simple breakfast of toasted bread, butter and jam or pho. Rooms were spacious & comfortable with a huge bed which looks like it had seen better days. Towels and sheets are nice and clean however. The front desk staffs are very helpful and helped us organise our tours and transportation. Room rates are very reasonable and wouldn't hesitate to recommend anyone looking for accommodation with a budget yet offering the services travellers need.

reviews.title: The hotel is clean and beautiful, staff is friendly 10 points.
reviews.text: Our family arrived early with two children, but we were allowed to check in early. The room is large, spacious, and beautiful, close to Poem Mountain. The hotel is near Co Nghi yogurt, and for dining, you need to travel 3-4 km to reach Hòn Gai beach for fun and then come back. For breakfast, there is pho and a buffet with fruits and coffee, all very delicious, and we can order extra food comfortably. The room is clean and beautiful, 10 out of 10. The hotel staff is always supportive, 100 points for their enthusiasm and friendliness.
